narrative:
This morning had the last "cool rain" you will see in a long, long time. We hope you enjoyed it. A lingering light shower persists into early afternoon, especially western slopes. We'll have partial sunshine by late afternoon. Temperatures stay seasonably cool with a breeze. Warmer weather builds Wednesday and Thursday. Wednesday is as pleasant as late June offers; heat and humidity arrive Thursday.

This week's Monday Message is now available for our subscribers. Severe weather potential for Monday, along with a return to classical summertime weather by next weekend are topics of discussion.

Leftover Light Shower; Cool

Last Updated Tue June 23 at 4:13PM by Scott Stephens

This morning had the last "cool rain" you will see in a long, long time. We hope you enjoyed it. A lingering light shower persists into early afternoon, especially western slopes. We'll have partial sunshine by late afternoon. Temperatures stay seasonably cool with a breeze. Warmer weather builds Wednesday and Thursday. Wednesday is as pleasant as late June offers; heat and humidity arrive Thursday.

Monday

Forecast Discussion

A cold front with an upper-level disturbance has pressed eastward across the mountains this morning. We have cool temperatures with early rain followed by stray, light showers later this morning. We'll have some sun later today. Temperatures remain cooler than average today with a noticeable NW breeze. Tonight has clearing skies and cool temperatures.

Wednesday, high pressure moves from the Ohio Valley to the Appalachians. We will have as nice a day as June can offer. Thursday is somewhat warmer and more humid. I cannot completely rule out a stray afternoon shower or thundershower Thursday.

Today's front washes out by Thursday, and the storm track shifts northward. High pressure in the Atlantic muscles into the Southeast. A front edges our way from the north this weekend, but it does not reach the Southern Appalachians. But the front, along with increasing humidity and energy to our north, combine to bring more unsettled weather Friday through the weekend. Showers and thunderstorms will be in play mostly in the afternoons and evenings Friday and Saturday. Some of these storms will be strong. Coverage ticks down slightly Sunday afternoon.

Early next week, warm, drier air builds into the Eastern U.S. We are headed to "full on" summer. Even with drier air, isolated heat-driven afternoon thunderstorms are possible.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for June 22, 2026

The week starts stormy, but ends up like the first full week of summer should, with hit-or-miss afternoon thundershowers and very warm temperatures. It's the Monday Message, available for our subscribers.

Jun 22, 2026
Yadkin Valley Deluge

A morning complex of thunderstorms moving across the Ohio Valley and into West Virginia and Virginia produced an outflow boundary that pushed southward into North Carolina during the afternoon of June 18. This outflow boundary, the result of a cool rush of air from thunderstorms occurring to the north, served as an effective focusing mechanism for training thunderstorms that occurred over parts of Surry, northern Wilkes and southern Alleghany County during Thursday afternoon and evening.

This occurred under the backdrop of increasing tropical moisture returning from the southwest as the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur moved into Alabama.

The result was excessive rainfall, in the range of 2-4 inches, with locally higher amounts of around 5 inches in central Surry County.

Jun 19, 2026
WWR? Wolf Laurel!
